rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into implementation scope when binding to subcomponent ports or implementation-level conditions.
Place input envelope assumptions and output guarantees in component type scope only when using visible, typed ports with concrete bounds.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by aligning AGREE annex scope with the component’s type or implementation context.
Only reference ports, features, or data types explicitly defined in the AADL model within AGREE annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` for all AGREE content; ensure it is nested within component type or implementation blocks.
Range constraints must use visible scalar ports or constants—not AADL properties or implicit types.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Avoid using subcomponent paths or implementation-only names in component type annexes.
